hoses: Large black hard suction hose from pond floating strainer to Engine 181 intake
Attack line: 200 ft of 1.75-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to firefighter operating on the rural grass fire
appliances: Floating strainer at end of hard suction hose
sceneElements.elevation: Level rural field operation, 0 PSI elevation correction for attack line

problems.title: Attack Line Pump Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is drafting from a pond and supplying one 200 ft 1.75-inch attack line with a ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle operating at 185 GPM @ 50 PSI. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pump pressure for the attack line is based on attack-line friction loss plus nozzle pressure. The hard suction, pond, and floating strainer are part of the intake side and are not added to the discharge pressure calculation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add hard suction or drafting setup components to the attack-line pump pressure. Use the attack hose friction loss plus nozzle pressure.

problems.title: Attack Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying 185 GPM through 200 ft of 1.75-inch attack hose. What is the attack line friction loss?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Round FL to nearest 5 PSI: 105 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: At 185 GPM through 200 ft of 1.75-inch hose, the friction loss rounds to 105 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not include nozzle pressure when the question asks only for friction loss.

problems.title: Total Flow
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating one ChiefXD 185 nozzle from the drafting setup. What is the total flow from Engine 181?
problems.instructorExplanation: Only one attack line is flowing, so the total flow is the rated flow of that nozzle: 185 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the hard suction as a separate discharge flow. It is the intake supply path from the pond to the pump.

problems.title: Static Lift Pressure
problems.question: If the water surface at the pond is 8 ft below the pump intake, what lift pressure must the drafting setup overcome?
formulaBreakdown: Lift pressure = lift height × 0.5 PSI per foot.
problems.instructorExplanation: The drafting setup must overcome the vertical lift from the water surface to the pump intake. At 8 ft of lift, the pressure equivalent is 4 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate lift from the length of the hard suction hose. Lift is based on vertical distance from the water surface to the pump intake.

problems.title: Static Water Duration
problems.question: If the accessible static water supply contains 12000 usable gallons and Engine 181 is flowing 185 GPM, how long will the water supply last?
problems.instructorExplanation: Water supply duration is the usable water volume divided by the operating flow. At 185 GPM, 12000 gallons lasts about 65 minutes.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use pump pressure to calculate water duration. Duration is based only on available gallons and flow.

problems.title: Additional Attack Line Flow
problems.question: If a second identical ChiefXD 185 attack line is added, what would the total flow from Engine 181 be?
formulaBreakdown: Total flow = flow per line × number of lines.
problems.instructorExplanation: Two identical ChiefXD 185 attack lines would flow a total of 370 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not change the nozzle flow because the engine is drafting. The total flow is based on the number of operating nozzles.

problems.title: Drafting Intake Setup
problems.question: How many hard suction hoses are shown drafting from the pond into Engine 181?
formulaBreakdown: The scene shows one hard suction hose connected from the pond to Engine 181.
Hard suction hoses drafting from pond = 1.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 is drafting through one large hard suction hose with a floating strainer at the water source.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count the attack hose as a drafting hose. The attack hose is connected to a discharge, while the hard suction is connected to the intake.
